title: Savory Flaky Light Filipino Sirloin Steak Spring Rolls
description: A Filipino-inspired dish featuring tender sirloin steak wrapped in a flaky and light spring roll pastry, perfect for a snack or appetizer. This recipe combines the richness of steak with the freshness of Filipino flavors.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, fish sauce, calamansi juice, salt, and black pepper.
Add the sliced sirloin steak to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Remove the steak from the marinade and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked to an internal temperature of 145°F.
Let the steak rest for 5 minutes before slicing into thin strips.
In a small bowl, mix together chopped onion and minced garlic.
Lay a spring roll pastry sheet on a flat surface and place a few slices of the cooked steak in the center of the pastry.
Add a small spoonful of the onion-garlic mixture on top of the steak.
Brush the edges of the pastry with a small amount of water and fold the bottom half of the pastry over the filling.
Fold in the sides and roll up the pastry to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients and serve immediately.
